steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Textual) question. It asks for the specific challenge the researchers had to overcome to examine the glyphs. The answer must be directly supported by a detail in the passage — not inferred or imported from outside.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The cave was so remote that the researchers couldn't easily reach it.": The passage never mentions the cave being remote or hard to access; this claim has no textual basis. ✗
  • "Some of the glyphs were so faint that they couldn't be photographed.": The passage says the glyphs couldn't be photographed in their entirety because of the low ceiling and the glyphs' width — not because they were faint. ✗
  • "The researchers were unable to create a 3D model of the cave.": The passage states the opposite: the researchers successfully used photogrammetry to assemble a 3D model. ✗
  • "The cave's dimensions prevented the researchers from fully viewing the glyphs.": The passage directly states the cave's ceiling was only a few feet high, affording no position from which the ten-foot-wide glyphs could be viewed in their entirety. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
